message:
fix(sse): harden SSE data accumulation with projected-length bounds check

Refactor the SSE parser's data line accumulation to prevent OOM from
malicious or broken streams by checking the projected buffer size
*before* appending rather than checking current size *after*:

- Extract append_data_line() helper with explicit max_event_data_bytes
  parameter and projected-length calculation using saturating arithmetic
  to prevent overflow
- Promote the 100MB limit to a named constant MAX_EVENT_DATA_BYTES
- Replace two inline data-append sites (field:value and bare "data"
  lines) with calls to the new helper
- The previous approach checked current.data.len() > limit after the
  data had already been appended to the buffer, meaning a single large
  line could push the buffer well past the limit. The new approach
  computes projected_len = current.len + value.len + 1 and skips the
  append entirely if it would exceed the cap.

New test: test_append_data_line_enforces_projected_limit verifies that
a second append is rejected when it would push the buffer over the
configured limit, using a tight 3-byte cap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
